# FE handoff ‚Äî recipe `story` field

**Date:** 2026-06-18
**TL;DR:** New optional field `story` on `Recipe`. Manually authored narrative paragraph (origin, family note, etc.). Nullable; not generated by AI. Doesn't replace the per-user personalized description ‚Äî they coexist.

---

## Backend contract

```ts
// RecipeOut now includes:
story: string | null   // 1..5000 chars when present, null otherwise

// RecipeCreate / RecipeUpdate accept (optional):
story?: string | null
```

**Constraints:**
- `null` = no story.
- If you send a string, it must be **1..5000 characters** (empty `""` is `422`).
- Same `story` value for every household member viewing the recipe ‚Äî distinct from `GET /cookbook/recipes/{id}/description` which is per-user.

**PATCH semantics:**
| You send | Effect |
| --- | --- |
| Key omitted from body | Story unchanged |
| `"story": null` | Clear story (set to NULL in DB) |
| `"story": "new text"` | Replace |
| `"story": ""` | **422** ‚Äî not allowed |

---

## What changes on the FE

1. **Recipe detail screen** ‚Äî render the story block when `recipe.story != null`. Hide the section entirely when null. Suggested placement: between `description` and `ingredients`, since that matches the "tell me about this recipe" reading order.

2. **Create-recipe form** (manual entry) ‚Äî add an optional multi-line text input labeled "Story" or "About this recipe". Leave it empty and the backend stores `null`.

3. **Edit-recipe form** ‚Äî same input, prefilled with current value. A clear button on the FE that sends `{"story": null}` to wipe it.

4. **AI generate / photo extract preview screens** ‚Äî no change needed. The preview shape doesn't include `story` (always `null` after these flows), but the user can type one on the review screen before they hit save.

---

## What does NOT change

- **AI generate** (`POST /cookbook/recipes/generate`): preview returns `story: null`. The cookbook agent is **not** producing stories. If product wants AI-generated stories later, it's a separate ticket.
- **Photo extract** (`POST /cookbook/recipes/extract-photo`): same ‚Äî no story extracted from photos.
- **Personalized description** (`GET /cookbook/recipes/{id}/description`): still its own endpoint, still per-user, still cached. Both story and description can be shown on the same screen.

---

## Story vs personalized description ‚Äî quick clarifier

| | Story | Personalized description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Source | Manually typed by recipe author | AI-generated per viewer |
| Scope | Same for every viewer | Different for each user |
| Endpoint to read | `GET /cookbook/recipes/{id}` (in `RecipeOut.story`) | `GET /cookbook/recipes/{id}/description` |
| Endpoint to write | `POST` / `PATCH /cookbook/recipes/{id}` | (not user-writable; auto-generated) |
| Can be null? | Yes, default null | No ‚Äî returns `""` on failure |

If you're showing both on the recipe detail screen, treat them as separate paragraphs with different headings ‚Äî e.g. "About this recipe" (story) and "Why you might like it" (personalized description).

---

## Quick test

```bash
TOKEN=...
BASE=...

# Create with story
curl -sS -X POST $BASE/cookbook/recipes \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "name": "Sunday pasta",
    "story": "My grandmother made this every Sunday.",
    "ingredients": [{"name":"pasta","quantity":"200","unit":"g","category":"grains"}]
  }'
# ‚Üí 201 with story echoed back

# Clear story
RID=...
curl -sS -X PATCH $BASE/cookbook/recipes/$RID \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"story": null}'
# ‚Üí 200 with story: null
```

---

## Related change shipping at the same time ‚Äî family members can edit their own recipes

`PATCH /cookbook/recipes/{id}` was admin-only. As of this release it accepts:

- **Admin** ‚Äî full edit including `status`.
- **Creator** (`recipe.submitted_by == caller.id`) ‚Äî any field **except** `status`. Works at any status (pending or approved). Edits go live immediately, no admin re-review.

Non-admin sending `{"status": "approved"}` ‚Üí 403 `{"detail":"Only admins can change a recipe's status"}`. Non-creator non-admin trying to PATCH someone else's recipe ‚Üí 403.

**FE impact:** if your recipe-detail screen hides the "Edit" button on recipes the user didn't submit, that's correct for non-creators. But on the user's own pending/approved submissions, the button should now be visible regardless of role.

Quick test:
```bash
# Family member edits their own pending recipe ‚Üí 200
curl -sS -X PATCH $BASE/cookbook/recipes/$MY_RECIPE_ID \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $FAMILY_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"name": "Renamed by me"}'

# Family member tries to flip status ‚Üí 403
curl -sS -X PATCH $BASE/cookbook/recipes/$MY_RECIPE_ID \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $FAMILY_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"status": "approved"}'
```

DELETE remains admin-only.

---

## Rollback

If anything breaks, the column is additive and non-required ‚Äî there's nothing to roll back on the FE. Backend can ignore the field temporarily by dropping it from `RecipeCreate` / `RecipeUpdate`; existing data stays put.

‚Äî Belal
